items: Do not allow brake fluid to adhere to painted surfaces. If it does adhere, wash it off with water immediately.
To prevent brake fluid from spilling out, the joints of the disconnected hoses should be plugged with a plug or the like.
The figure shows a vehicle with 12-inch brakes.

blocks.text: Remove the oil bolt (A). Disconnect the brake hose (B) from the caliper body (C). Hold the caliper pin (D) with a wrench and remove the flange bolt (E). Remove the caliper body.
Remove the pin boot (C) from the groove of caliper pin A and caliper pin B. Remove the caliper pin from the caliper bracket (D). Remove the pin boot from the caliper bracket.

items: Do not allow brake fluid to adhere to painted surfaces. If it does adhere, wash it off with water immediately.
To prevent brake fluid from spilling out, the joints of the disconnected hoses should be plugged with a plug or the like.

blocks.text: Apply grease to the sliding parts of caliper pin A and B and inside the new pin boot (C). Install the pin boot. Install caliper pin A and pin B on the caliper bracket (D). Securely install the pin boot into the caliper bracket groove (E) and caliper pin groove (F).

items: For vehicles equipped with 12-inch brakes, Nigroove RM made by Nippon Grease shall be applied.
For vehicles equipped with 13-inch brakes, Cosmo rubber grease must be applied.
Do not allow dust, etc. to adhere to the sliding parts of the pins.
The caliper pins are different at the top and bottom. Check the installation position of caliper pin A and caliper pin B.

blocks.text: Attach the caliper body (A) to the caliper bracket (B). Hold the caliper pin (C) with a wrench and tighten the flange bolt (D) to the specified torque. Install the brake hose (E) with the oil bolt (F) and a new sealing washer (G).
After installation, inspect the brake hose for kinks, interference with other parts, or fluid leakage.
